tool.cpp(3) Library Functions Manual tool.cpp(3)

NAME

tool.cpp

SYNOPSIS

Functions


std::mutex & outputRegistryMutex ()
std::unordered_set< std::string > & outputRegistry ()
bool registerOutputPath (const std::string &path)
void unregisterOutputPath (const std::string &path)
int main (int argc, const char **argv)

Function Documentation

int main (int argc, const char ** argv)

Definition at line 813 of file defer/tool.cpp.

813                                       {
814   InitLLVM InitLLVM(argc, argv);
815 
816   // Capture CWD before anything else can change it
817   g_originalCwd = fs::current_path();
818 
819   // Hide all the LLVM internal options that aren't relevant to our tool
820   cl::HideUnrelatedOptions(ToolCategory);
821 
822   cl::ParseCommandLineOptions(argc, argv, "ascii-defer transformation tool\n");
823 
824   fs::path outputDir = fs::path(OutputDirectoryOption.getValue());
825   // Make output directory absolute relative to original CWD
826   if (!outputDir.is_absolute()) {
827     outputDir = g_originalCwd / outputDir;
828   }
829   fs::path inputRoot;
830   if (!InputRootOption.getValue().empty()) {
831     inputRoot = fs::path(InputRootOption.getValue());
832   } else {
833     inputRoot = fs::current_path();
834   }
835 
836   // Make input root absolute for reliable path computation
837   if (!inputRoot.is_absolute()) {
838     std::error_code ec;
839     inputRoot = fs::absolute(inputRoot, ec);
840     if (ec) {
841       llvm::errs() << "Failed to resolve input root path: " << ec.message() << "\n";
842       return 1;
843     }
844   }
845 
846   std::vector<std::string> sourcePaths;
847   for (const auto &path : SourcePaths) {
848     if (!path.empty()) {
849       sourcePaths.push_back(path);
850     }
851   }
852 
853   if (sourcePaths.empty()) {
854     llvm::errs() << "No translation units specified for transformation. Provide positional source paths.\n";
855     return 1;
856   }
857 
858   if (fs::exists(outputDir)) {
859     if (!fs::is_directory(outputDir)) {
860       llvm::errs() << "Output path exists and is not a directory: " << outputDir.c_str() << "\n";
861       return 1;
862     }
863   } else {
864     std::error_code errorCode;
865     fs::create_directories(outputDir, errorCode);
866     if (errorCode) {
867       llvm::errs() << "Failed to create output directory: " << outputDir.c_str() << " - " << errorCode.message()
868                    << "\n";
869       return 1;
870     }
871   }
872 
873   // Load compilation database
874   std::string buildPath = BuildPath.getValue();
875   if (buildPath.empty()) {
876     buildPath = ".";
877   }
878   std::string errorMessage;
879   std::unique_ptr<tooling::CompilationDatabase> compilations =
880       tooling::CompilationDatabase::loadFromDirectory(buildPath, errorMessage);
881   if (!compilations) {
882     llvm::errs() << "Error loading compilation database from '" << buildPath << "': " << errorMessage << "\n";
883     return 1;
884   }
885 
886   tooling::ClangTool tool(*compilations, sourcePaths);
887 
888   // Build the list of arguments to prepend for system header resolution
889   // LibTooling uses CC1 mode internally which has different include path handling than
890   // the clang driver. We use -Xclang to pass CC1-level flags that properly configure
891   // system include paths for LibTooling's CompilerInvocation.
892   std::vector<std::string> prependArgs;
893 
894   // Try to find clang resource directory at runtime
895   // Priority: 1) CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR compile-time path, 2) Runtime detection via common paths
896   std::string resourceDir;
897 
898 #ifdef CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR
899   if (llvm::sys::fs::exists(CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR)) {
900     resourceDir = CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR;
901     // llvm::errs() << "Using embedded clang resource directory: " << resourceDir << "\n";
902   } else {
903     if (!QuietMode) {
904       llvm::errs() << "Embedded clang resource directory not found: " << CLANG_RESOURCE_DIR << "\n";
905     }
906   }
907 #endif
908 
909   // Runtime detection if embedded path doesn't work
910   if (resourceDir.empty()) {
911     // Common locations for clang resource directories
912     std::vector<std::string> searchPaths;
913 
914 #ifdef __APPLE__
915     // Homebrew LLVM on Apple Silicon
916     searchPaths.push_back("/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/lib/clang");
917     // Homebrew LLVM on Intel Mac
918     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/local/opt/llvm/lib/clang");
919     // Xcode's clang
920     searchPaths.push_back(
921         "/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/lib/clang");
922     // CommandLineTools clang
923     searchPaths.push_back("/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/lib/clang");
924 #endif
925 #ifdef __linux__
926     // System LLVM installations
927     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/llvm-22/lib/clang");
928     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/llvm-21/lib/clang");
929     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/llvm-20/lib/clang");
930     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/lib/clang");
931     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/local/lib/clang");
932 #endif
933     // Universal fallback
934     searchPaths.push_back("/usr/local/lib/clang");
935 
936     for (const auto &basePath : searchPaths) {
937       if (!llvm::sys::fs::exists(basePath)) {
938         continue;
939       }
940 
941       // Find the highest version subdirectory
942       std::error_code ec;
943       std::string bestVersion;
944       int bestMajor = 0;
945 
946       for (llvm::sys::fs::directory_iterator dir(basePath, ec), dirEnd; !ec && dir != dirEnd; dir.increment(ec)) {
947         std::string name = llvm::sys::path::filename(dir->path()).str();
948         // Parse version number (e.g., "22", "21.1.0", "21")
949         int major = 0;
950         if (sscanf(name.c_str(), "%d", &major) == 1 && major > bestMajor) {
951           bestMajor = major;
952           bestVersion = dir->path();
953         }
954       }
955 
956       if (!bestVersion.empty()) {
957         resourceDir = bestVersion;
958         if (!QuietMode) {
959           llvm::errs() << "Found clang resource directory at runtime: " << resourceDir << "\n";
960         }
961         break;
962       }
963     }
964 
965     if (resourceDir.empty() && !QuietMode) {
966       llvm::errs() << "Warning: Could not find clang resource directory\n";
967     }
968   }
969 
970   // Disable default system includes - we'll add them back explicitly in the correct order
971   // This prevents clang's builtin path from shadowing SDK system headers
972   prependArgs.push_back("-nostdinc");
973 
974   if (!resourceDir.empty()) {
975     prependArgs.push_back(std::string("-resource-dir=") + resourceDir);
976   }
977 
978   // Add target triple - LibTooling needs this to validate architecture-specific flags
979   // Without a target, flags like -mavx2 cause "unsupported option for target ''" errors
980 #ifdef __APPLE__
981 #ifdef __arm64__
982   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
983   prependArgs.push_back("arm64-apple-darwin");
984   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: arm64-apple-darwin\n";
985 #else
986   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
987   prependArgs.push_back("x86_64-apple-darwin");
988   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: x86_64-apple-darwin\n";
989 #endif
990 #elif defined(__linux__)
991 #ifdef __aarch64__
992   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
993   prependArgs.push_back("aarch64-linux-gnu");
994   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: aarch64-linux-gnu\n";
995 #else
996   prependArgs.push_back("-target");
997   prependArgs.push_back("x86_64-linux-gnu");
998   // llvm::errs() << "Using target: x86_64-linux-gnu\n";
999 #endif
1000 #endif
1001 
1002   // Override the sysroot for macOS. Homebrew's LLVM config file sets -isysroot
1003   // to CommandLineTools SDK, but we strip that from compile_commands.json and
1004   // set our own explicitly to ensure consistent behavior.
1005   std::string selectedSDK;
1006 #ifdef __APPLE__
1007   {
1008     const char *sdkPaths[] = {
1009         // Xcode SDK (preferred - most complete headers and frameworks)
1010         "/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/"
1011         "MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk",
1012         // CommandLineTools SDK (fallback for users without Xcode)
1013         "/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk",
1014     };
1015 
1016     for (const char *sdk : sdkPaths) {
1017       if (llvm::sys::fs::exists(sdk)) {
1018         selectedSDK = sdk;
1019         break;
1020       }
1021     }
1022 
1023     if (!selectedSDK.empty()) {
1024       prependArgs.push_back("-isysroot");
1025       prependArgs.push_back(selectedSDK);
1026       // llvm::errs() << "Using macOS SDK: " << selectedSDK << "\n";
1027     } else {
1028       llvm::errs() << "Warning: No macOS SDK found, system headers may not be available\n";
1029     }
1030   }
1031 #endif
1032 
1033   // Build list of system include paths to add as -isystem paths.
1034   // LibTooling (cc1 mode) doesn't automatically add system include paths like
1035   // the clang driver does, so we add them explicitly:
1036   // We use -isysroot to let cc1 mode find SDK headers, and DON'T add clang's builtin
1037   // include path explicitly since cc1 mode searches it automatically and it would
1038   // shadow the system headers when included with -isystem.
1039   std::vector<std::string> appendArgs;
1040   // Note: We skip adding resourceDir/include as -isystem because cc1 mode
1041   // searches the clang resource directory automatically BEFORE -isystem paths,
1042   // which would cause it to look for stdio.h in the wrong place.
1043 
1044   // NOTE: We rely on -isysroot (set in prependArgs) to find system headers.
1045   // LibTooling on LLVM 21 has a bug where __has_include() evaluates even in non-taken
1046   // preprocessor branches and creates VFS entries for non-existent files (ptrcheck.h).
1047   // By using -isysroot alone without explicitly adding -isystem paths, we avoid
1048   // triggering this bug while still allowing cc1 mode to find system headers.
1049 
1050   // Single consolidated argument adjuster that:
1051   // 1. Preserves compiler path (first arg)
1052   // 2. Inserts prepend args immediately after compiler (-nostdlibinc, -resource-dir, -isysroot)
1053   // 3. Strips unnecessary flags from remaining args
1054   // 4. Adds system include paths as -isystem at the end (after project -I paths)
1055   // 5. Adds defer tool parsing define BEFORE the "--" separator
1056   std::string inputRootStr = inputRoot.string();
1057   auto consolidatedAdjuster = [prependArgs, appendArgs, inputRootStr](const tooling::CommandLineArguments &args,
1058                                                                       StringRef) {
1059     // Helper to check if a path is under the project root
1060     auto isProjectPath = [&inputRootStr](const std::string &path) -> bool {
1061       if (inputRootStr.empty())
1062         return false;
1063       // Normalize both paths for comparison
1064       std::error_code ec;
1065       auto normalizedPath = fs::canonical(path, ec);
1066       if (ec)
1067         return false; // Path doesn't exist or can't be resolved
1068       auto normalizedRoot = fs::canonical(inputRootStr, ec);
1069       if (ec)
1070         return false;
1071       // Check if the path starts with the root
1072       std::string pathStr = normalizedPath.string();
1073       std::string rootStr = normalizedRoot.string();
1074       if (pathStr.find(rootStr) != 0)
1075         return false;
1076       // Exclude .deps-cache directory - these are cached dependencies that use
1077       // angled includes (<header.h>) and need -isystem, not -iquote
1078       if (pathStr.find("/.deps-cache/") != std::string::npos)
1079         return false;
1080       return true;
1081     };
1082     tooling::CommandLineArguments result;
1083 
1084     if (args.empty()) {
1085       return result;
1086     }
1087 
1088     // First: preserve the compiler path (first argument)
1089     result.push_back(args[0]);
1090 
1091     // Second: add the prepend args right after the compiler
1092     for (const auto &arg : prependArgs) {
1093       result.push_back(arg);
1094     }
1095 
1096     // Third: process remaining arguments, stripping unnecessary flags
1097     // IMPORTANT: Convert -I to -iquote for project include paths.
1098     // Clang include search order for <header.h>:  -I paths -> -isystem paths -> system paths
1099     // Clang include search order for "header.h":  -iquote paths -> -I paths -> -isystem paths -> system paths
1100     //
1101     // The problem: Project -I paths are searched BEFORE -isystem paths for <stdio.h>.
1102     // This causes LibTooling to look for <stdio.h> in the project's lib/ directory first.
1103     //
1104     // The fix: Convert project -I to -iquote. Then <stdio.h> skips project paths entirely
1105     // and finds system headers in -isystem paths instead.
1106     //
1107     // ALSO: Collect all -isystem paths and reorder them so clang builtins come FIRST.
1108     // LibTooling on LLVM 21 has a VFS bug where it creates phantom entries for headers
1109     // in -isystem directories even when the header doesn't exist there.
1110     std::vector<std::string> collectedIsystemPaths;
1111     bool foundSeparator = false;
1112     size_t separatorIndex = 0;
1113     for (size_t i = 1; i < args.size(); ++i) {
1114       const std::string &arg = args[i];
1115 
1116       // When we hit "--", note its position and break
1117       if (arg == "--") {
1118         foundSeparator = true;
1119         separatorIndex = i;
1120         break;
1121       }
1122 
1123       // Skip sanitizer flags
1124       if (arg.find("-fsanitize") != std::string::npos)
1125         continue;
1126       if (arg.find("-fno-sanitize") != std::string::npos)
1127         continue;
1128       // Skip debug info flags (not needed for AST parsing)
1129       if (arg == "-g" || arg == "-g2" || arg == "-g3")
1130         continue;
1131       if (arg == "-fno-eliminate-unused-debug-types")
1132         continue;
1133       if (arg == "-fno-inline")
1134         continue;
1135       // Strip -resource-dir flags and their arguments - we added our embedded path
1136       if (arg == "-resource-dir") {
1137         ++i;
1138         continue;
1139       }
1140       if (arg.find("-resource-dir=") == 0)
1141         continue;
1142       // Strip -isysroot flags and their arguments - we added our embedded SDK path
1143       if (arg == "-isysroot") {
1144         ++i;
1145         continue;
1146       }
1147       if (arg.find("-isysroot=") == 0 || (arg.find("-isysroot") == 0 && arg.length() > 9))
1148         continue;
1149 
1150       // Collect -isystem paths instead of passing them through
1151       // We'll add them at the end in the correct order (clang builtins first)
1152       if (arg == "-isystem" && i + 1 < args.size()) {
1153         collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(args[++i]);
1154         continue;
1155       }
1156       if (arg.find("-isystem") == 0 && arg.length() > 8) {
1157         collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(arg.substr(8));
1158         continue;
1159       }
1160 
1161       // Convert -I to -iquote for all project include paths
1162       // Convert -I to -isystem for dependency paths (so they come after our clang builtins)
1163       // This prevents <stdio.h> and <stdbool.h> from being searched in project directories
1164       // before system include paths.
1165       //
1166       // LibTooling's cc1 mode searches -I paths BEFORE -isystem paths for <stdio.h>, which
1167       // causes it to look in the project's include directory before system headers. By converting
1168       // all project -I to -iquote, <stdio.h> will skip project paths and find the system header.
1169       //
1170       // For public API headers like <ascii-chat/header.h>, using -iquote works fine because:
1171       // -iquote paths come first in the search order for quoted includes, and quoted includes
1172       // can find both public and private headers.
1173       if (arg == "-I" && i + 1 < args.size()) {
1174         // -I /path/to/dir (separate argument)
1175         const std::string &includePath = args[++i];
1176         if (isProjectPath(includePath)) {
1177           // All project paths (including public include/) - convert to -iquote
1178           result.push_back("-iquote");
1179           result.push_back(includePath);
1180         } else {
1181           // Collect dependency -I paths to add as -isystem after our builtins
1182           collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(includePath);
1183         }
1184         continue;
1185       }
1186       if (arg.find("-I") == 0 && arg.length() > 2) {
1187         // -I/path/to/dir (combined form)
1188         std::string includePath = arg.substr(2);
1189         if (isProjectPath(includePath)) {
1190           // All project paths (including public include/) - convert to -iquote
1191           result.push_back("-iquote");
1192           result.push_back(includePath);
1193         } else {
1194           // Collect dependency -I paths to add as -isystem after our builtins
1195           collectedIsystemPaths.push_back(includePath);
1196         }
1197         continue;
1198       }
1199 
1200       result.push_back(arg);
1201     }
1202 
1203     // Fourth: add system include paths in the correct order
1204     // Order matters for LibTooling on LLVM 21 - clang builtins MUST come first
1205     // to shadow any phantom VFS entries that might be created for other paths
1206     for (const auto &arg : appendArgs) {
1207       result.push_back(arg);
1208     }
1209     // Then add the collected -isystem paths from the compilation database
1210     for (const auto &path : collectedIsystemPaths) {
1211       result.push_back("-isystem");
1212       result.push_back(path);
1213     }
1214 
1215     // Fifth: add the defer tool define and separator
1216     result.push_back("-DASCIICHAT_DEFER_TOOL_PARSING");
1217     if (foundSeparator) {
1218       result.push_back("--");
1219       // Copy any remaining args after "--"
1220       for (size_t i = separatorIndex + 1; i < args.size(); ++i) {
1221         result.push_back(args[i]);
1222       }
1223     }
1224 
1225     return result;
1226   };
1227   tool.appendArgumentsAdjuster(consolidatedAdjuster);
1228 
1229   DeferActionFactory actionFactory(outputDir, inputRoot);
1230   const int executionResult = tool.run(&actionFactory);
1231   if (executionResult != 0) {
1232     llvm::errs() << "Defer transformation failed with code " << executionResult << "\n";
1233   }
1234   return executionResult;
1235 }

  std::string > & outputRegistry ()

  path)

46   auto &registry = outputRegistry();

48   return inserted;

void
  unregisterOutputPath (const std::string & path)
